artifacts.summary: 4-phase implementation plan for Python best-first search with neural value network scoring and A/B comparison runner

memory_candidates.content: When integrating a neural network scorer into an existing rule-based search system, the Python-drives-search architecture (Option A) is necessary when the target system (Lean REPL) cannot callback into Python during execution. The pattern is: reimplement the search loop in Python with the rule-based heuristic as baseline, add the neural component as an additive bonus with configurable weight (alpha) and temperature scaling, and validate baseline parity against the original system before enabling the neural component. Define a typing.Protocol for the value network interface to decouple testing from the actual model implementation.
